observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary journal entry to reclassify the balance of cash donations received amounting to P7,800,000.00 from the Due to NGAs account to Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and properly record future financial assistance received for disaster related purposes in the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and ensure timely compliance or implementation of programs/purpose intended by the grant, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary journal entries to remove from PPE accounts those that fall under the classification of semi-expendable items, with a net book value of P2,070,184.38 and close the related Accumulated Depreciation to the appropriate accounts.
Moreover, succeeding purchases of properties with costs of P50,000.00 and above should be recognized as PPE while tangible items with costs below the capitalization threshold should be recorded under the appropriate semi-expendable property account.
Finally, we recommended that the required forms, registries and reports be prepared and maintained by the General Services Officer in order to establish accountability over these items and to safeguard them from the risk of improper use and possible loss or theft.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly record the granting of financial assistance to individuals under the Donations account instead of the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management undertake the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of receivables to ensure their accuracy and existence;
For dormant accounts where records and documents are no longer available, conduct an investigation as to the causes of the missing documents or to identify the officials and employees responsible; and
Initiate the request for write-off of the dormant accounts and comply with guidelines and procedures on the proper disposition and write-off/derecognition of dormant accounts outlined in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately draw the necessary journal entries to recognize the transfer of the procured equipments from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books upon issuance of the same to the end user and compute the corresponding depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation.

We recommended that the Management implement corrective measures and/or conduct repairs on the noted scaling of road pavement in the following infrastructure projects:
Concreting of Road from Cataratan Barangay Hall to Cataratan Integrated School (Brgy. Cataratan, Allacapan);
Upgrading of Farm to Market Road (Brgy. San Juan, Allacapan); and
Upgrading of Farm to Market Road (Brgy. Tubel, Allacapan).
Moreover, we required that the Management submit the Statement of Work Accomplished as of April 21, 2022 of the Concreting of Road going to Evacuation Center project for the computation of the Liquidated Damages due to the delays in its completion.
Finally, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Engineering Office to supervise the implementation of the infrastructure projects to avoid any deficiency in the infrastructure projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management submit the lacking documents on the payment of mobilization fee, progress billing/final payment and release of retention money for the Construction of Health Center at Capagaran, Concreting of Farm-to-Market Road at Labben and Capanickian Sur projects and ensure that disbursements for infrastructure projects of the Municipality are supported with complete documentary requirements in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks and require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and to furnish the Accounting Office with the original Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher, following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.

Secure monthly bank statements to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements to determine whether checks claimed by the payees were presented for payment, and secure the addresses of the payees for notification of the return of stale checks in their possession for replacement.

Draw the JEV to restore the cash and liabilities pertaining to the stale checks to present fairly the recorded Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer immediately discontinue the payment of regular expenses out of the Petty Cash Fund and ensure that Petty Cash Funds should only be used for cash payment of non-recurring expenses, which could not conveniently be paid in checks or which require immediate payment, in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks and require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Accounting Office with the original Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.
Secure Bank Statements in order to regularly prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements to determine whether checks claimed by the payees were presented for payment. Likewise, secure the addresses of the payees for notification of the return of stale checks in their possession for replacement.
Draw the JEV to restore the cash and liabilities pertaining to the stale checks to present fairly the recorded Cash in Bank and accounts payable accounts.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the documents supporting the reconciling items. Consequently, we also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make representation with the concerned bank for verification and proper disposition of the same. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all bank reconciling items are properly supported with complete documents and transmit a copy of the monthly BRS to concerned banks, pursuant COA Circular 96-011.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials and employees to comply with COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 to ensure the fair presentation of the Local Road Network account in the Financial Statements.
Consequently, we recommended that the LCE:
Require the conduct of inventory of the local roads under the LGUs jurisdiction and the reconciliation of the list of inventory per Local Road Inventory Report and Road Map with those recorded in the Road Networks account. Thereafter, the preparation of Local Road Network Report must be observed yearly; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to properly disclose the details of the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in the format prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Vouchers to recognize the various completed infrastructure projects funded from the fund transfers in accordance with PPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Management restore the unspent balance of LDRRMF for Capital Outlay amounting to P260,390.00 from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books and strictly comply with the accounting guidelines and disclosures of LDRRMF under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for the fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended that the Management, thru the GFPS Executive Committee and TWG, comply with PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 on the timely implementation of the programs, projects and activities under the GAD Plan and extend to the constituents, particularly to the women and children, their immediate needs as enumerated under the Magna Carta of Women. We also recommended that the Management conduct a periodic review and updating of their GPB to address local needs, concerns and welfare of women, including other gender related issues.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the General Services Officer to submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels and ensure that the driver’s trip tickets are serially numbered and properly accomplished. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant charge the fuel consumptions of the heavy equipment to the specific projects of the Municipality for fair presentation of the account in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Management request from the payee the Official Receipts on the payments made for loading fees (gravel) in accordance with COA Circular No.  2004-006 dated September 9, 2004 on the acceptability of evidence of receipt of payment of disbursements. We also recommended that a MOA be executed between the Municipality and the private permitee/concessionaire to establish the obligations and commitments of both parties.  Likewise, direct payment should be considered since the amounts involved are material.

We recommended management to notify and direct all concerned department who are receiving goods in-kind from National Government Agencies or from any source, to inform and coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO), for the receipt and inspection of such goods, before issuance to the concerned department or end user.  The GSO shall likewise furnish the accounting department the necessary records on the receipt and issuance for proper recording in the books of accounts.  The accounting office shall make the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts to effect the corrections on unrecorded transactions pursuant to IPSAS 23.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up the medical/dental/laboratory supplies for distribution as inventories upon purchase/receipt thereof and the corresponding expense accounts only upon distribution to present fairly the accounts in the books of the LGU for the benefit of all users of the information. Further, she should prepare and submit journal entry vouchers (JEVs) taking up periodic adjustments for the undistributed balance of the same, if any and the erroneous recording of various purchases of medical, dental and laboratory supplies.
observations.managementAction: Procurement of medical, dental and laboratory supplies were recognized as inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to stop the grant of honorarium more than the allowable rate and strictly observe the provisions of LBC Nos. 62 and 102 in the granting of honorarium to national government officials assigned in the local government unit.
observations.managementAction: Honoraria granted was already within the allowable amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management increase the fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer to cover the maximum accountability that he handles for a given period, in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Bonds provided by the Bureau of Treasury, so as to protect the interest of the LGU in case of losses resulting from unlawful use of government funds and losses attributable to negligence in the safekeeping of funds.
observations.managementAction: The fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer was already increased, sufficient to cover his accountability.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should designate a disbursing officer who shall exclusively perform the disbursing functions pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 50 of GAAM, Volume III. The designated employee should not act as collector at the same time to avoid possible irregularities and its concealment.
observations.managementAction: The Petty Cash Custodian has already been designated solely as disbursing officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned departments to strictly follow and implement the provisions of the above-mentioned Circular to fairly present the book value of the Road Networks account in the Financial Statements of the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were not complied.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer made a partial settlement amounting to P264,374.17.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that collections and deposits should be recorded daily in the cashbooks to enable the accountable officers to reconcile cashbook balance with the cash on hand at the close of each day.  Likewise, reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records every end of the month and take up immediately the necessary corrections in the books requiring adjustments to reconcile both the cashbook and the ledger pursuant to Item 7, Chapter II of the Revised Cash Examination Manual, and Sections 26 of the New Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume II thereby set up internal control to forestall the possibility of loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The cashbook were updated regularly and reconciled with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Stop the practice of assigning the tasks of collecting officers to personnel hired as job orders/contractual/casuals and maximize the services of the present permanent revenue collection officers to protect the interest of the government;

Require the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in the recovery of the unremitted collections or hold the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer personally liable and accountable for the loss from the collection of such job order.

Require also the Liquidating Officer to be cautious in the verification of all collections and accountable forms turned over to her thus enhance control techniques in the handling of cash and accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: The Management already minimizes assigning job orders as collectors. Also, the Liquidating Officer is already cautious in the verification of collections and accountable forms turned over to her.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Cash Advances for Payrolls and Petty Cash Funds be granted directly to the duly designated disbursing officers and refrain from transferring cash advances among accountable officers pursuant to Section 4.1.5 and Section 4.1.6 of COA Circular 97-002, thereby exercising proper safeguards to protect government funds.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances for payroll and petty cash fund were already granted directly in the name of duly designated Disbursing Officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month and disclose the details of the unexpended balance in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund was regularly submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management implement strictly all the priority development projects as planned in order to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in the municipality and eventually deliver timely benefits to its constituents. Also, submit explanation or justification why these projects were not implemented.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, several projects were implemented by the Municipality as of year-end based on the submitted accomplishment report by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and Municipal Environment and Natural Resource Office to oversee the full implementation of the Solid Waste Management PPAs of LGU Allacapan to timely avail of the benefits that the public could derive therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had utilized 71% of its appropriated fund for Solid Waste Management in the implementation of its projects/program for CY 2020, based on the accomplishment report submitted.

observations.recommendation: To ensure the timely implementation of the programs, projects and activities under the Gender and Development Fund and to extend to the constituents particularly the women and children their immediate needs as enumerated under the Magna Carta of Women Law, we recommended management thru the GFPS Executive Committee and TWG to comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 in the implementation of GPB.   Periodic review and corrections should be made on GPB to address local needs, concerns and welfare of women including other gender related issues.
observations.managementAction: Based on the accomplishment report submitted by the management, most projects programmed under the GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2020 were implemented as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Officer to prepare the necessary documents as basis of the Sangguniang Bayan  (SB) to pass  a resolution effecting the transfer to the recipient barangays. The Municipal Accountant will draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) based on the approved SB resolution and Deed of Donation duly executed between the municipality and recipient barangays.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality undertake the following measures to regulate the granting of financial assistance:

Require the Sangguniang Bayan to enact a resolution prescribing the policies and guidelines on the grant of donations/financial assistance  containing the following:

Kind of financial assistance (medical, educational, funeral, etc.)
Approval limits for specific cases, including on the frequency of grants to each individual
Criteria in the granting of donation and the documentary requirements for each particular assistance
All cases should be referred to the MSWDO for eligibility evaluation and the case study report prepared should be made part of the supporting documents of the claim.

See to it that applicable laws, rules and regulations, particularly Section 2, 4 (6) of P.D.1445 and Section 335 of R. A. 7160 are observed in the disbursement of public funds to avoid disallowance in audit.

Appropriated funds for financial assistance/donations should be fully transferred to the MSWDO.

Refrain from charging financial assistance against Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses account because this practice depletes the budget for operating activities of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU already established a well-defined policy and guidelines on the grant of financial assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management implement the SALINTUBIG Fund with urgency considering that these are priority projects. Make representation with the Department of Health as the funding source for possible continuance of the abandoned project. Impose liquidated damages on the contractor Camia Construction for the delayed completion of the project if warranted. Management is also enjoined to comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations with regard to the utilization of financial assistance to ensure and promote delivery of services to the constituents of the locality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU thru the Municipal Engr. conferred with the DOH (in-charge with SALINTUBIG) the status of the project, however, clear guidelines as to the continuation/stoppage of the project were not resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit its staffing pattern and consider the funding of the created positions.  In this regard, the LGU may fill up its vacant plantilla positions and reduce the number of job order workers, thus, establish more accountability at work.

Make strategic action plan on the proper scheduling in the implementation of the programmed projects /programs to ensure its full implementation  as well as utilize the fund budgeted thereof.
Review whether or not the prior years’ unimplemented projects are still appropriate for the needs of the constituents.
Ensure that adequate funds are available for the implementation of the continuing and current years’ development programs.

observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendation.

Design/adopt measures and strategies to remedy the weak internal control on the management of the waterworks system by instituting a well-defined operational guidelines/policy on the operation of the Municipality’s Waterworks System. 
Revisit Ordinance No. 2017-4 on the Municipal Charges/Fees on the availment/use of the waterworks in order to address the areas where the municipal government can generate additional income and strictly imposed the same for the implementation of the priority projects, programs and activities of the municipality to the benefit of its constituents.

Maximize personnel holding permanent positions to do regular, administrative and clerical functions.
Specify project that necessitate the employment of job orders and ensure approval by the Sanggunian Bayan.

Limit the hiring of job order employees based on the approved annual budget.
